content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

July 10, 2008

Karen M. Steentofte
General Counsel
Sacramento County Office of Education
10474 Mather Blvd.
P.O. Box 269003
Sacramento, CA 95826

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. W-08-098

Dear Ms. Steentofte:

This letter is in response to your request for written advice on the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹ Your question concerns the duties of Sacramento County Superintendent of Schools David Gordan. Your question is a follow up to advice received in the Steentofte Advice Letter, No. I-08-047.

As we discussed in our telephone conversations, your follow up questions call for a determination and evaluation of facts as applied to the law set forth in the prior letter. The Fair Political Practices Commission does not act as a finder of fact when it renders assistance. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) Therefore, we decline to provide further advice.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

JWW:jgl

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109 – 18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
